message.content:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user's primary request was to implement a complete remote deployment flow via Railway API from their dashboard (`apps/web`). This involved:
   - Wiring up Railway's GraphQL API to deploy agent containers
   - Adding Telegram bot configuration to the configure step
   - Reordering the flow: Configure → Deploy → Fund (removing the separate "Create Wallet" step since the container self-registers with Locus)
   - After deployment + funding, showing a monitoring dashboard with USDC balance, mock Lido staking, APY, and daily yield projections
   - Fixing agent crash loops caused by missing `/data` directory and Locus API rate limiting
   - Fixing Telegram configuration to match the working approach from `builders-garden/farclaw` and `builders-garden/farcon-claw` repos
   - Storing Claude Code conversation transcripts in the repo for hackathon submission with auto-sync hooks

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - Railway GraphQL API v2 (`https://backboard.railway.com/graphql/v2`) — mutations: `serviceCreate`, `serviceConnect`, `variableCollectionUpsert`, `volumeCreate`, `serviceDomainCreate`; queries: `deployment`, `deployments`, `deploymentLogs`
   - Locus beta self-registration API (`https://beta-api.paywithlocus.com/api/register`) — 5 registrations per IP per hour rate limit
   - OpenClaw agent configuration via `openclaw.json` — `channels.telegram.dmPolicy` set to `"allowlist"` with `allowFrom` array for no-manual-pairing Telegram setup
   - Railway services within a shared project (`RAILWAY_PROJECT_ID`) — one project, many agent services
   - Persistent volumes at `/data` for agent state, Locus credentials, OpenClaw config
   - Mock Lido staking with 3.3% APY, simulated yield accrual (10s ticks = ~1 hour accelerated)
   - USDC on Base (`0x833589fCD6eDb6E08f4c7C32D4f71b54bdA02913`)
   - Claude Code hooks (`PreToolUse` on `Bash` matcher) for auto-syncing conversations before commits
   - `envsubst` for rendering OpenClaw config templates at container boot

3. Files and Code Sections:

   - **`apps/web/src/lib/railway.ts`** (Created, then rewritten)
     - Railway GraphQL API client used by deploy routes
     - Final version uses `serviceConnect` (not `serviceInstanceUpdate` with `source`), `variableCollectionUpsert` (bulk, not per-variable), `getLatestDeployment` with `projectId`
     - Removed unused `createProject` and `triggerDeploy` functions
     ```typescript
     export async function createService(
       projectId: string,
       name: string,
       repo: string,
       branch: string = "main",
       rootDirectory: string = "apps/agent"
     ): Promise<{ serviceId: string; environmentId: string }>
     
     export async function setEnvVars(
       projectId: string,
       serviceId: string,
       environmentId: string,
       vars: Record<string, string>
     ): Promise<void>  // uses variableCollectionUpsert
     
     export async function getLatestDeployment(
       projectId: string,  // was missing before
       serviceId: string,
       environmentId: string
     ): Promise<{ id: string; status: string } | null>
     ```

   - **`apps/web/src/app/api/agent/deploy/route.ts`** (Rewritten multiple times)
     - POST endpoint that creates Railway service, sets env vars, creates volume + domain
     - Uses shared `RAILWAY_PROJECT_ID`, service name = agent name for uniqueness
     - Passes `LOCUS_API_KEY` from server env if available
     - Telegram vars: frontend input → server env fallback → skip
     ```typescript
     const locusApiKey = process.env.LOCUS_API_KEY;
     if (locusApiKey) {
       envVars.LOCUS_API_KEY = locusApiKey;
     }
     const telegramToken = body.telegramBotToken || process.env.TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN;
     const telegramUsers = body.telegramAllowedUsers || process.env.TELEGRAM_ALLOWED_USERS;
     ```

   - **`apps/web/src/app/api/agent/deploy/status/route.ts`** (Created)
     - GET endpoint polling deployment status, extracts wallet address from logs
     - Regex matches `[init]   Wallet:   0x...` from openclaw-init.sh output
     ```typescript
     const match = line.match(/Wallet:\s+(0x[a-fA-F0-9]{40})/i);
     ```

   - **`apps/web/src/components/deploy-agent.tsx`** (Rewritten)
     - 4-step flow: Configure → Deploy → Fund → Monitor
     - Configure step includes Telegram Bot Token (password field) + Allowed User IDs
     - After funding, auto-transitions to AgentMonitor component
     ```typescript
     const STEPS = [
       { id: "configure", label: "Configure" },
       { id: "deploy", label: "Deploy" },
       { id: "fund", label: "Fund" },
       { id: "monitor", label: "Monitor" },
     ] as const;
     ```

   - **`apps/web/src/components/agent-monitor.tsx`** (Created)
     - Post-funding monitoring dashboard
     - 4-card grid: USDC balance, Lido stETH stake, APY (3.3%), daily yield
     - Mock Lido staking with % quick-select buttons (25/50/75/100%)
     - Simulated yield accrual every 10 seconds
     - Top-up USDC via real on-chain transaction
     - Yield projection: accrued, daily, monthly, annual + inference calls/day estimate

   - **`apps/web/src/hooks/use-agent-deploy.ts`** (Rewritten)
     - Removed `registerWallet`, `pollWalletStatus`, `checkBalance`
     - Added `deployAgent`, `pollDeployStatus`, `getAgentWallet`
     ```typescript
     interface DeployInfo {
       projectId: string;
       serviceId: string;
       environmentId: string;
       domain: string;
       setupPassword: string;
     }
     ```

   - **`apps/agent/openclaw-init.sh`** (Edited multiple times)
     - Added `mkdir -p /data` before credential write
     - Replaced single-shot Locus registration with 5 retries + exponential backoff
     - Removed `exit 1` on missing inference provider
     - Added HTTP status code + response body logging for Locus errors
     - Added `envsubst` to render `openclaw.template.json` → `$OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR/openclaw.json`
     - Added Node.js patch script for Telegram allowlist mode in `openclaw.json`
     ```bash
     # Retry with backoff
     BACKOFF=5
     for attempt in $(seq 1 5); do
       REG_RESPONSE="$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -X POST ...)"
       HTTP_CODE="$(echo "$REG_RESPONSE" | tail -1)"
       REG_RESPONSE="$(echo "$REG_RESPONSE" | sed '$d')"
       if [ "$HTTP_CODE" = "200" ] || [ "$HTTP_CODE" = "201" ]; then break; fi
       sleep "$BACKOFF"
       BACKOFF=$((BACKOFF * 2))
     done
     ```
     ```bash
     # Render OpenClaw config from template (first boot only)
     if [ ! -s "$OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR/openclaw.json" ]; then
       mkdir -p "$OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR"
       envsubst < /app/custom/config/openclaw.template.json > "$OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR/openclaw.json"
     fi
     ```
     ```bash
     # Telegram allowlist patch via Node.js (matching farclaw approach)
     TELEGRAM_ALLOWED_USERS="${TELEGRAM_ALLOWED_USERS:-}" node <<'PATCH_EOF'
     const config = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(configPath, "utf8"));
     const users = (process.env.TELEGRAM_ALLOWED_USERS || "").split(",").map(s => s.trim()).filter(Boolean);
     if (users.length > 0) {
       config.channels.telegram.dmPolicy = "allowlist";
       config.channels.telegram.allowFrom = users;
     }
     PATCH_EOF
     ```

   - **`apps/agent/config/openclaw.template.json`** (Edited)
     - Added missing `channels.telegram` section
     ```json
     "channels": {
       "telegram": {
         "enabled": true,
         "dmPolicy": "paired",
         "allowFrom": []
       }
     }
     ```

   - **`apps/web/.env.local.example`** (Edited)
     - Added `RAILWAY_API_TOKEN`, `RAILWAY_PROJECT_ID`, `LOCUS_API_KEY`, `TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN`, `TELEGRAM_ALLOWED_USERS`

   - **`conversations/sync.sh`** (Created, then rewritten for portability)
     - Auto-detects Claude project dir from repo path using sanitized path
     - Copies .jsonl transcripts to `conversations/`

   - **`.claude/settings.json`** (Created)
     - Shared PreToolUse hook for auto-syncing conversations before git commits
     ```json
     {
       "hooks": {
         "PreToolUse": [{
           "matcher": "Bash",
           "hooks": [{
             "type": "command",
             "command": "jq -r '.tool_input.command' | grep -q '^git commit' && ./conversations/sync.sh && git add conversations/ 2>/dev/null || true",
             "timeout": 30,
             "statusMessage": "Syncing conversations..."
           }]
         }]
       }
     }
     ```

   - **`.gitignore`** (Edited)
     - Added `.claude/`, `!.claude/settings.json`, `.agents/`

4. Errors and fixes:
   - **Railway API `serviceInstanceUpdate` with `source` field**: Was invalid — `ServiceInstanceUpdateInput` doesn't accept `source`. Fixed by using `serviceConnect` mutation for repo/branch linking.
   - **`setEnvVars` had hardcoded empty `projectId: ""`**: Would fail at runtime. Fixed by removing the broken function and consolidating into one `setEnvVars` that takes `projectId`.
   - **`variableUpsert` triggering N redeploys**: Each call triggers a redeploy. Fixed by using `variableCollectionUpsert` for bulk set in single API call.
   - **`DeploymentListInput` missing `projectId`**: Required field was omitted. Fixed by adding `projectId` parameter to `getLatestDeployment`.
   - **Agent crash loop — `/data` directory missing**: `openclaw-init.sh` line 60 tried to write `/data/.locus-credentials.json` but `/data` didn't exist. Fixed with `mkdir -p /data` early in script.
   - **Agent crash loop — Locus 429 rate limit**: Single-shot registration with `curl -sf` swallowed errors. Crash loop burned through 5 registrations/hour limit. Fixed by: (1) logging HTTP status + response body, (2) 5 retries with exponential backoff (5s→80s), (3) never `exit 1` — agent boots and waits.
     - User feedback: "no it should be register with agent only" — when I tried to pre-register from dashboard to avoid rate limiting, user wanted registration to stay in the agent container.
   - **Railway services not getting env vars from `.env`**: User asked "why railway deployment is not getting LOCUS_API_KEY from .env even if it's there?" — Railway services don't inherit local env, must be explicitly set via API. Fixed by adding passthrough in deploy route.
   - **Telegram not working — missing OpenClaw config**: Compared with farclaw/farcon-claw repos and found: (1) `openclaw.template.json` had no `channels.telegram` section, (2) template was never rendered into `openclaw.json`, (3) `TELEGRAM_ALLOWED_USERS` only exported as env var but never patched into config. Fixed all three.
   - **`.claude/` and `.agents/` tracked in git**: User noticed these on GitHub. Fixed by adding to `.gitignore` and `git rm -r --cached`.
   - **Conversation sync script hardcoded path**: Initially used `-Users-limone-Documents-builders-garden-synthesis-hack`. Fixed by auto-detecting from repo path using sanitized directory name.

5. Problem Solving:
   - Solved Railway API correctness by researching docs (web search) and comparing with actual API schema
   - Solved agent crash loop through iterative debugging: first `/data` missing, then Locus rate limiting
   - Solved Telegram configuration by studying working implementations in farclaw/farcon-claw repos
   - Solved conversation persistence by creating portable sync script + shared Claude Code hook
   - Wallet address extraction from deployment logs uses regex matching `Wallet:\s+(0x[a-fA-F0-9]{40})` based on actual init script output

6. All user messages:
   - "Implement the following plan: [detailed Railway deployment plan]"
   - "why i need railway github repo or project id? i'm expecting multiple users deploying their agent while using our infra"
   - "maybe project id makes sense, so all this agents are services inside a single project?"
   - "what else we missing for a full real deployment?"
   - "push to main"
   - "got stuck here for this error [crash loop screenshots]"
   - "yes push"
   - "still getting this [Locus registration failure screenshots]"
   - "https://docs.paywithlocus.com/quickstart-beta read again here and see if we are doing anything weird"
   - "it's a 429 [rate limit screenshot]"
   - "push this to main"
   - "no it should be register with agent only"
   - "why railway deployment is not getting LOCUS_API_KEY from .env even if it's there?"
   - "make it so if telegram variable on frontend are empty, they fallback on env variables, if no env variables, then no telegram channel connection no problem"
   - "did we set the telegram env variable for openclaw correctly as in /builders-garden/farclaw or /builders-garden/farcon-claw?"
   - "pull from remote"
   - "pull from remote, as my cofounder pushed a similar fix to telegram"
   - "are we storing the ownerPrivateKey in the local volume on docker / railway? the one coming from locus self-agent creation / where are we storing our conversations"
   - "can we store conversations of this repo, we need them for the hackathon"
   - "can we set a hook on claude locally for this project to push local conversations to git right before any commit?"
   - "can we set the pretooluse hook on the repo so that also applies to my cofounder?"
   - "okay now, after deployment is ready, other than deposit of usdc, mock the stake to Lido, and the dashboard should show current balance of an agent in USDC + current stake on Lido, APY and"

## Synthesis Hackathon with Celo

According to Celo's official announcement on Twitter dated March 10, 2026, developers are invited to build agentic apps with real-world utility, leveraging Celo's robust infrastructure and tooling. Winners stand to earn up to $10,000 in bounties. Participants will utilize Celo's infrastructure and tools, with the platform's agent serving as a judge.

## Self Protocol

Self lets you verify your identity while keeping your data private, and you can prove who you are, your age, where you're from and verify it onchain or offchain. Using the Self.xyz SDK, a user can disclose their country, age (or range), that they are not on OFAC or from a sanctioned country, and verify that proof on-chain on the Celo network. To be eligible, projects must implement Self onchain SDK (right now only on Celo mainnet and testnet).

## Celo Hackathon Categories

Categories in Celo hackathons include mobile banking, loyalty/rewards program, payments at merchants, microinsurance, microlending, prediction markets, mobile gaming, and cross-border remittances.

## SF Agentic Commerce x402 Hackathon

SKALE Network, in partnership with Google, Coinbase, Virtuals, Edge & Node, and Pairpoint by Vodafone, announced the San Francisco Agentic Commerce x402 Hackathon. 

**Event Details:**
- The three-day hybrid event runs February 11-13, 2026, with $50,000 in prizes for developers building real-world applications that enable agents to transact autonomously
- Projects will be judged by industry leaders in agentic commerce, with $50,000 in prizes awarded across overall awards and sponsor bounties

- The total prize pool is valued at $50,000 across overall awards and sponsor bounties
- Build tracks and prize categories will be announced soon

**Coinbase's Role:**
Participants will have access to Coinbase's x402 standard for internet-native payments, along with infrastructure from other partners including SKALE, Google Cloud, Virtuals, Edge & Node, and Vodafone.

The registration page is available at https://dorahacks.io/hackathon/x402 for developers interested in participating.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Self Protocol, the SDK, bounties, hackathons, Celo, and identity verification in 2026:

## Self Protocol Overview

Self Protocol is a new identity verification system leveraging zero-knowledge proofs, launched by Self Labs. Self Labs was founded by former Celo core team members and acquired OpenPassport.

## SDK and Features

The Self.xyz SDK allows users to disclose their country, age (or range), that they are not on OFAC or from a sanctioned country, and verify that proof on-chain on the Celo network. Users can prove who they are, their age, where they're from and verify it onchain or offchain.

## Hackathon Involvement

SelfProtocol has been announced as a sponsor for the 'Build Agents for the Real World' hackathon, and there are bounty opportunities available for developers integrating the protocol.

## Recent 2026 Development

Google Cloud has integrated Self Protocol's zero-knowledge proof-of-humanity into its Web3 faucet for the Celo Sepolia testnet, intending to curb automated abuse and redirect test tokens toward verified developers.

The protocol addresses privacy-preserving identity verification needs in the blockchain ecosystem while enabling Sybil-resistant applications.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Talent Protocol's bounty, synthesis, hackathon, builder score, and identity systems:

## Talent Protocol Overview

Talent Protocol is a groundbreaking initiative in professional reputation management within the blockchain ecosystem, serving as an open, verifiable system designed to highlight and reward individuals actively 'building' in the Web3 space.

## Builder Score System

The Builder Score is a single, uncapped numerical value that serves as a real-time reflection of your on-chain professional reputation, with the goal to distinguish true builders—those who invest time and effort to create value—from passive users.

The protocol uses three categories of credentials: Identity, Activity, and Skills, each representing a different type of reputation data.

## Credentials & Identity

Identity credentials contribute a maximum of 20 points to a user's Builder Score, and if a user's Identity credentials reach 20 or higher, they receive a "Human Checkmark" on their profile.

Activity credentials help identify trusted actors and value-add members, capped at 40 points maximum.

Skills credentials highlight onchain builders and their specific areas of expertise, with no cap—all skills credentials count towards your Builder Score.

## Hackathons & Building

Winning an ETHGlobal hackathon is a strong signal that someone is a real builder. Participation in hackathons and platforms like ETHGlobal or Base Buildathons can add significant points under the Activity category.

## Synthesis Hackathon

Synthesis is an online hackathon judged by AI agent judges across the Ethereum ecosystem, and Talent Protocol is listed as one of the partners.

Built on HTTP. · GitHub","url":"https://github.com/coinbase/x402"},{"title":"What is the x402 Payment Protocol?","url":"https://blog.quicknode.com/x402-protocol-explained-inside-the-https-native-payment-layer/"},{"title":"What is x402? | Payment Protocol for AI Agents on Solana","url":"https://solana.com/x402/what-is-x402"},{"title":"How to Implement a Crypto Paywall with x402 Payment Protocol | Quicknode Guides","url":"https://www.quicknode.com/guides/infrastructure/how-to-use-x402-payment-required"},{"title":"Welcome to x402 - Coinbase Developer Documentation","url":"https://docs.cdp.coinbase.com/x402/welcome"},{"title":"Introducing x402 V2: Evolving the Standard for Internet-native Payments","url":"https://www.x402.org/writing/x402-v2-launch"},{"title":"WEPIN Blog | Payment Rails for the Age of AI Agents: Analyzing the x402 Protocol","url":"https://www.wepin.io/en/blog/ai-agent-payment-infrastructure-x402-protocol"},{"title":"Open Payment Standard x402 Expands Capabilities in Major Upgrade - InfoQ","url":"https://www.infoq.com/news/2026/01/x402-agentic-http-payments/"}]

Based on the search results, here's comprehensive information about the x402 protocol:

## What is x402?

x402 is an open, neutral standard for internet-native payments that makes payments possible between clients and servers, creating win-win economies that empower agentic payments at scale. It's an open payments protocol developed by Coinbase that enables AI agents to complete transactions autonomously.

## How It Works

x402 is built-in to existing HTTP requests, with no additional communication required. Here's the basic flow:

1. If a request arrives without payment, the server responds with HTTP 402, prompting the client to pay and retry.
2. The client selects one of the PaymentRequirements returned by the server response and creates a PaymentPayload based on the scheme & network of the PaymentRequirements they have selected. The client sends the HTTP request with the PAYMENT-SIGNATURE header containing the PaymentPayload to the resource server.
3. The resource server verifies the PaymentPayload is valid either via local verification or by POSTing the PaymentPayload and PaymentRequirements to the Network.

## Agent Payments

By enabling machine-native payments, x402 enables AI agents to autonomously discover and procure third-party cloud resources, contextual data, and API tools—making it easier for them to achieve their targeted optimization goals without human-in-the-loop intervention.

Agents will pay for services, fund their compute, manage subscriptions, and take action on behalf of their users in an increasingly independent fashion. This shift signals the rise of agentic payments, a new layer of the internet economy where machine to machine transactions make up a growing portion of services demand.

## Payment Features

x402 removes the need for API keys, accounts, and subscriptions and enables true pay-per-use access without subscriptions, prepaid credits, or manual invoicing. With near-zero transaction costs, payments as low as $0.001 cents per request are possible.

## Self Protocol and Celo Synthesis Hackathon

The Self.xyz SDK allows users to disclose their country, age (or range), that they are not on OFAC or from a sanctioned country, and verify that proof on-chain on the Celo network.

### Hackathon Details

Celo is participating in the Synthesis agent hackathon, encouraging developers to create agentic applications with real-world utility. Participants will utilize Celo's infrastructure and tools, with the platform's agent serving as a judge. Winners stand to earn up to $10,000 in bounties.

### Bounty Requirements

The requirement is to integrate Self onchain SDK into your application so users can prove their identity, with instructions available in the docs to understand how to integrate the Self SDK into your Smart Contract.

The hackathon specifically mentions this is "an event dated March 10, 2026, where developers are invited to build agentic apps with real-world utility, leveraging Celo's robust infrastructure and tooling."

Celo is participating in the Synthesis agent hackathon, encouraging developers to create agentic applications with real-world utility. Participants will utilize Celo's infrastructure and tools, with the platform's agent serving as a judge. Winners stand to earn up to $10,000 in bounties.

### Bounty Requirements

The requirement is to integrate Self onchain SDK into your application so users can prove their identity, with instructions available in the docs to understand how to integrate the Self SDK into your Smart Contract.

The hackathon specifically mentions this is "an event dated March 10, 2026, where developers are invited to build agentic apps with real-world utility, leveraging Celo's robust infrastructure and tooling."

content.text:
Now I have a solid picture. Here's my analysis:

---

## Synthesis Hackathon — Microlending + Self + Celo: Bounty Compatibility

### Your Concept (from your notes)

A **microlending platform** where:
1. **Registration**: Privy wallet → Self identity verification → human verified (8004)
2. **Lending**: x402 endpoints for borrow/repay with identity-verified signatures
3. **Reputation**: Automated reviews feed back into identity scores

### Highly Compatible Bounties

| Sponsor | Est. Prize | Why It Fits |
|---------|-----------|-------------|
| **Celo** | Up to $10k | Primary chain. Self SDK only works on Celo mainnet/testnet. Microlending is an explicit Celo hackathon category. |
| **Self (self.xyz)** | TBD | Core requirement: integrate Self onchain SDK for KYC (age, country, OFAC, human verification). Your registration flow maps directly to this. |
| **x402 (Coinbase)** | TBD | Your borrow/repay endpoints use HTTP 402 payment-required pattern — agent-to-agent microlending over x402 is a perfect fit. |
| **Locus** | TBD | Already in your stack. Agent payments infrastructure — agents paying for services or repaying loans via Locus wallets. |
| **Bond.Credit** | TBD | Listed as Synthesis partner — literally a credit protocol. Your microlending use case aligns directly. |
| **Talent Protocol** | TBD | Builder Score as reputation input. Identity credentials (max 20pts) + Activity credentials could feed into your credit scoring. |

### Stretch / Secondary Bounties

| Sponsor | Angle |
|---------|-------|
| **Ampersend** | Payment rails — could complement x402 for the lending settlement layer |
| **Lido DAO** | You already have Lido staking in the agent. Collateralized lending against stETH yield is a natural extension. |
| **Venice** | Already your inference provider. If they have an AI bounty, your automated review/reputation system qualifies. |
| **Base** | x402 supports Base. If lending contracts deploy on Base, you could qualify for both Celo + Base bounties. |

### Architecture That Maximizes Bounties

```
User Registration (Self + Privy + Celo)
├── Privy creates embedded wallet
├── Self SDK verifies identity (passport/age/country/OFAC) → onchain on Celo
├── Human verification (8004) → Sybil resistance
└── Talent Protocol Builder Score → credit reputation input

Microlending (x402 + Bond.Credit + Locus)
├── x402 endpoint: POST /borrow → HTTP 402 → borrower pays fee → loan issued
├── x402 endpoint: POST /repay → HTTP 402 → repayment settled
├── Self identity verification on recipient side (lender verifies borrower)
├── Locus smart wallet for agent-managed repayments
└── Bond.Credit integration for credit scoring/terms

Collateral & Yield (Lido + Celo)
├── stETH staking as collateral for microloans
├── Yield accrual offsets loan interest
└── Automated liquidation if collateral drops
```

### Key Technical Requirements to Hit Multiple Bounties

1. **Deploy on Celo** (mainnet or Alfajores testnet) — required for Self SDK
2. **Integrate Self onchain SDK** — `SelfVerificationRoot` contract + frontend QR flow
3. **x402 payment endpoints** — use Coinbase's x402 standard for borrow/repay HTTP flows
4. **Talent Protocol API** — fetch Builder Score as credit reputation input
5. **Locus agent wallets** — already built, use for autonomous loan management

### My Recommendation

This pivot is **very strong** — you'd realistically target **4-5 bounties** (Celo, Self, x402, Locus, Bond.Credit) with a single coherent product. The Self + Celo combination is especially well-aligned since Self only works on Celo and they're co-sponsoring.

Want me to start planning the implementation? The main new pieces would be:
- Self SDK integration (contract + frontend)
- x402 borrow/repay endpoints
- Credit scoring logic (Self identity + Talent Builder Score)

Sources:
- [Celo joins Synthesis Hackathon](https://blockchain.news/flashnews/celo-joins-synthesis-hackathon-to-promote-agentic-app-development)
- [Self Protocol - ETHGlobal bounty reference](https://ethglobal.com/events/taipei/prizes/self-protocol)
- [Self Protocol docs - Basic Integration](https://docs.self.xyz/contract-integration/basic-integration)
- [x402 - Internet-Native Payments Standard](https://www.x402.org/)
- [x402 Coinbase docs](https://docs.cdp.coinbase.com/x402/welcome)
- [Talent Protocol Builder Score](https://docs.talentprotocol.com/docs/protocol-concepts/scoring-systems/builder-score)
- [Locus - AI agent payment infrastructure](https://events.ycombinator.com/agenticpaymentshackathon)
- [Synthesis Hackathon](https://synthesis.md/hack/)

1→# Yield Agent — Self-Funded Autonomous AI
     2→
     3→An autonomous AI agent that funds its own operations entirely from stETH yield — no human tops up, no principal at risk.
     4→
     5→## Problem
     6→
     7→Today, running an AI agent costs money and requires constant human intervention to fund wallets, manage budgets, and approve payments. Agents can't be truly autonomous if someone has to keep feeding them.
     8→
     9→## Solution
    10→
    11→A user stakes ETH into a treasury contract backed by wstETH. The contract enforces a hard separation: the principal is structurally locked, and only accrued yield flows into the agent's spendable balance. The agent uses that yield to pay for its own inference, API calls, and services — fully autonomously.
    12→
    13→## How it works
    14→
    15→1. **Fund** — User deposits wstETH into the Agent Treasury contract. Configures spending permissions (recipient whitelist, per-tx cap, time windows).
    16→2. **Think** — Agent reasons privately via Venice's no-data-retention inference API. Sensitive treasury decisions never leave the private compute layer.
    17→3. **Swap** — Agent converts yield to USDC or VVV via Uniswap — paying for services or Venice inference directly.
    18→4. **Pay** — Agent pays for services through Locus wallets on Base — auditable, capped, autonomous.
    19→5. **Dashboard** — Web UI to deploy agents, stake ETH, monitor yield accrual, track spending, and adjust permissions.
    20→
    21→## Key properties
    22→
    23→- Principal is never accessible to the agent — enforced at the contract level
    24→- Agent spending is bounded by yield + configurable permissions
    25→- Private reasoning via Venice — the agent's strategy stays confidential
    26→- All payments flow through Locus — auditable, on-chain, USDC on Base
    27→
    28→## Integrated providers
    29→
    30→- [Lido](https://lido.fi) — wstETH staking and yield generation
    31→- [Venice](https://venice.ai) — Private, no-data-retention AI inference
    32→- [Uniswap](https://uniswap.org) — On-chain swaps (yield to USDC/VVV)
    33→- [Locus](https://locus.finance) — Agent-native payment infrastructure on Base
    34→

# Privy Agentic Wallets Implementation Guide

## Overview
The documentation provided outlines a high-level recipe for implementing agentic wallets but **does not include specific API endpoints, SDK method signatures, or code examples**. The page references external documentation links rather than providing technical implementation details directly.

## What's Available in the Source Document

### Control Models
Two approaches are described:
1. **Agent-controlled wallets**: "Your application backend controls the wallet via authorization keys" for fully autonomous systems
2. **User-owned with agent signers**: Users maintain ownership while granting scoped permissions to agents

### Implementation Steps Outlined

The framework includes six steps without code:
- Creating authorization keys via the Privy Dashboard
- Setting up key quorums for multi-party approval
- Defining policies (transfer limits, contract allowlists, recipient restrictions, time controls)
- Creating wallets with `owner_id` and `policy_ids` parameters
- Executing transactions through Privy's API
- Monitoring via webhooks (transaction and balance events)

## Critical Limitation

This page **explicitly directs developers to external documentation** for actual implementation:
- Authorization keys: "/controls/authorization-keys/owners/types#authorization-keys"
- Wallet creation: "/wallets/wallets/create/create-a-wallet#nodejs"
- Transaction execution: Multiple chain-specific endpoints referenced

**To obtain complete technical details, code examples, and API specifications, you must access the full documentation index at:** `https://docs.privy.io/llms.txt`

The source material functions as a conceptual guide rather than a technical reference.

1→FROM coollabsio/openclaw:latest
     2→
     3→# Install utilities (Debian-based image)
     4→RUN apt-get update && \
     5→    DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
     6→      curl jq gettext-base openssl && \
     7→    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
     8→
     9→# Copy config template and init script
    10→COPY config/ /app/custom/config/
    11→COPY workspaces/ /app/custom/workspaces/
    12→COPY openclaw-init.sh /app/custom/openclaw-init.sh
    13→RUN chmod +x /app/custom/openclaw-init.sh
    14→
    15→EXPOSE 8080
    16→
    17→HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=5s --start-period=30s --retries=3 \
    18→  CMD curl -f http://localhost:8080/healthz || exit 1
    19→
    20→ENTRYPOINT ["/app/custom/openclaw-init.sh"]
    21→

1→# Agent
     2→AGENT_NAME=yield-agent
     3→SETUP_PASSWORD=changeme
     4→
     5→# OpenClaw gateway (auto-generated at boot if empty)
     6→OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN=
     7→
     8→# Venice AI (optional — agent will bootstrap a key via VVV staking)
     9→# If set, Venice is used immediately for private inference.
    10→# If empty, Locus-wrapped OpenAI is used until Venice key is acquired.
    11→VENICE_MODEL=venice/llama-3.3-70b
    12→VENICE_API_KEY=
    13→
    14→# Locus (optional — agent self-registers at boot if empty)
    15→# If empty, the agent calls POST /api/register on first start.
    16→# Credentials are saved to /data/.locus-credentials.json and reused on restart.
    17→# Fund the wallet with USDC on Base after first boot (claim URL shown in logs).
    18→LOCUS_API_KEY=
    19→
    20→# Telegram channel (OpenClaw Telegram gateway)
    21→# Create a bot via @BotFather, paste the token here.
    22→# DM policy: "pairing" (default) requires approval, "allowlist" skips pairing for listed users.
    23→# To find your user ID: DM the bot, then run `openclaw logs --follow` and read from.id.
    24→TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN=
    25→TELEGRAM_DM_POLICY=allowlist
    26→TELEGRAM_ALLOW_FROM=
    27→

1→# Lido Agent Yield
     2→
     3→A smart contract protocol on **Base** that lets humans deposit [wstETH](https://docs.lido.fi/guides/lido-tokens-integration-guide/#wsteth) and allocate a configurable share of accrued staking yield to an AI agent wallet.
     4→
     5→## How it works
     6→
     7→1. A **YieldVaultFactory** (singleton) deploys lightweight [EIP-1167](https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-1167) clones — one vault per user / team.
     8→2. The vault **owner** (human) deposits wstETH and configures:
     9→   - **Agent wallet** — the address that receives yield.
    10→   - **Yield share** — percentage (basis points) of accrued yield the agent may withdraw.
    11→   - **Withdrawal frequency** — cooldown between agent withdrawals (e.g. 7 days for a weekly budget).
    12→3. Yield is **never rebased** into extra tokens. Instead it is computed on every call via the **Chainlink wstETH/stETH exchange rate oracle** on Base:
    13→
    14→   ```
    15→   yield_stETH  = vault_wstETH_balance * current_rate  -  principalStETH
    16→   yield_wstETH = yield_stETH / current_rate
    17→   ```
    18→4. The **agent** can withdraw up to `yieldShareBps %` of accrued yield, subject to the cooldown. It can never touch the principal.
    19→5. The **owner** has full control: withdraw principal, withdraw yield, update settings, pause, or emergency-exit at any time.
    20→
    21→## Key addresses (Base Mainnet)
    22→
    23→| Contract | Address |
    24→|---|---|
    25→| wstETH (ERC20Bridged) | `0xc1CBa3fCea344f92D9239c08C0568f6F2F0ee452` |
    26→| Chainlink wstETH/stETH exchange rate | `0xB88BAc61a4Ca37C43a3725912B1f472c9A5bc061` |
    27→
    28→## Project structure
    29→
    30→```
    31→src/
    32→  YieldVault.sol            # Per-user vault (clone implementation)
    33→  YieldVaultFactory.sol     # Factory that deploys vault clones
    34→  interfaces/
    35→    IAggregatorV3.sol        # Chainlink price feed interface
    36→test/
    37→  YieldVault.t.sol           # Fork tests (Base mainnet)
    38→script/
    39→  Deploy.s.sol               # Deployment script
    40→```
    41→
    42→## Prerequisites
    43→
    44→- [Foundry](https://book.getfoundry.sh/getting-started/installation)
    45→- A Base RPC endpoint (public `https://mainnet.base.org` works for testing)
    46→
    47→## Setup
    48→
    49→```bash
    50→cp .env.example .env
    51→# edit .env with your RPC URL
    52→
    53→source .env
    54→```
    55→
    56→## Build
    57→
    58→```bash
    59→forge build
    60→```
    61→
    62→## Test
    63→
    64→Tests run against a Base mainnet fork and use `vm.mockCall` to simulate oracle rate changes (yield accrual).
    65→
    66→```bash
    67→forge test --fork-url $BASE_RPC_URL -vvv
    68→```
    69→
    70→## Deploy
    71→
    72→```bash
    73→forge script script/Deploy.s.sol --rpc-url $BASE_RPC_URL --broadcast --verify
    74→```
    75→
    76→## Security considerations
    77→
    78→- **Oracle staleness** — reverts if the Chainlink answer is older than 25 hours (heartbeat + 1 h buffer).
    79→- **Reentrancy** — all state-changing functions use a reentrancy guard.
    80→- **Emergency exit** — `exit()` bypasses the pause flag so the owner can always recover funds.
    81→- **Factory pause** — protocol admin can halt all vault operations globally; individual vault owners can pause their own vault independently.
    82→- **No `receive()` / `fallback()`** — vaults only accept wstETH via `deposit()`.
    83→

1→# Safety & Guardrails
     2→
     3→## Financial Safety
     4→- Never spend more than your per-transaction cap
     5→- Never exceed your daily spending limit
     6→- Always confirm balance before sending payments
     7→- Never share your private key or API credentials
     8→
     9→## Operational Safety
    10→- Do not take irreversible actions without confirmation
    11→- Log all significant decisions and their reasoning
    12→- If uncertain, reduce spending and wait for guidance
    13→- Report errors immediately via the feedback endpoint
    14→
    15→## Privacy
    16→- Use Venice AI for all inference (no data retention)
    17→- Do not leak wallet keys, API keys, or sensitive data
    18→- Keep financial reasoning private
    19→

1→# Yield Agent
     2→
     3→You are an autonomous AI agent that funds its own operations entirely from stETH yield.
     4→
     5→## Core Behavior
     6→
     7→- You manage your own budget via your Locus wallet on Base (USDC)
     8→- You pay for inference, API calls, and services autonomously
     9→- You never touch the principal — only spend yield
    10→- You reason privately via Venice AI (no data retention)
    11→- You swap yield to USDC/VVV via Uniswap when needed
    12→
    13→## Wallet
    14→
    15→- Your wallet is managed via Locus on Base
    16→- All payments go through Locus with spending controls enforced
    17→- Check your balance regularly and operate within your budget
    18→
    19→## Decision Making
    20→
    21→- Always check balance before making payments
    22→- Log every transaction with a clear memo
    23→- Prioritize cost-efficient operations
    24→- Report status periodically via the Locus feedback endpoint
    25→

1→#!/bin/bash
     2→set -e
     3→
     4→echo "============================================"
     5→echo "  OpenClaw Agent Init"
     6→echo "============================================"
     7→
     8→# Defaults
     9→export AGENT_NAME="${AGENT_NAME:-yield-agent}"
    10→export VENICE_MODEL="${VENICE_MODEL:-venice/llama-3.3-70b}"
    11→export VENICE_API_KEY="${VENICE_API_KEY:-}"
    12→export LOCUS_API_KEY="${LOCUS_API_KEY:-}"
    13→export SETUP_PASSWORD="${SETUP_PASSWORD:-changeme}"
    14→export AUTH_PASSWORD="${SETUP_PASSWORD}"
    15→export OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR="${OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR:-/data/.openclaw}"
    16→export OPENCLAW_WORKSPACE_DIR="/data/workspace-agent"
    17→
    18→# Ensure /data exists (volume mount may not create it on first boot)
    19→mkdir -p /data
    20→
    21→LOCUS_CREDS_FILE="/data/.locus-credentials.json"
    22→
    23→# Generate gateway token if not provided
    24→if [ -z "${OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN:-}" ]; then
    25→  export OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN="$(openssl rand -hex 32)"
    26→  echo "[init] Generated OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN"
    27→fi
    28→
    29→# ── Locus self-registration ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
    30→# If no LOCUS_API_KEY, register with Locus beta to get a wallet + API key
    31→if [ -z "$LOCUS_API_KEY" ]; then
    32→  # Check if we have saved credentials from a previous boot
    33→  if [ -f "$LOCUS_CREDS_FILE" ]; then
    34→    echo "[init] Found saved Locus credentials from previous boot"
    35→    LOCUS_API_KEY="$(jq -r '.apiKey' "$LOCUS_CREDS_FILE")"
    36→    LOCUS_OWNER_KEY="$(jq -r '.ownerPrivateKey' "$LOCUS_CREDS_FILE")"
    37→    LOCUS_WALLET="$(jq -r '.ownerAddress' "$LOCUS_CREDS_FILE")"
    38→    export LOCUS_API_KEY
    39→  else
    40→    echo "[init] No LOCUS_API_KEY set — self-registering with Locus beta..."
    41→    REG_RESPONSE=""
    42→    BACKOFF=5
    43→    for attempt in $(seq 1 5); do
    44→      HTTP_CODE=""
    45→      REG_RESPONSE="$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -X POST https://beta-api.paywithlocus.com/api/register \
    46→        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    47→        -d "{\"name\": \"$AGENT_NAME\"}" 2>&1)"
    48→      HTTP_CODE="$(echo "$REG_RESPONSE" | tail -1)"
    49→      REG_RESPONSE="$(echo "$REG_RESPONSE" | sed '$d')"
    50→
    51→      if [ "$HTTP_CODE" = "200" ] || [ "$HTTP_CODE" = "201" ]; then
    52→        echo "[init] Locus responded with HTTP $HTTP_CODE"
    53→        break
    54→      fi
    55→
    56→      echo "[init] Registration attempt $attempt/5 failed (HTTP $HTTP_CODE)"
    57→      echo "[init] Response body: $REG_RESPONSE"
    58→      echo "[init] Retrying in ${BACKOFF}s..."
    59→      sleep "$BACKOFF"
    60→      BACKOFF=$((BACKOFF * 2))
    61→      REG_RESPONSE=""
    62→    done
    63→
    64→    if [ -z "$REG_RESPONSE" ]; then
    65→      echo "[init] WARNING: Locus registration failed after 5 attempts"
    66→      echo "[init] Agent will boot without Locus — set LOCUS_API_KEY manually to fix"
    67→    fi
    68→
    69→    if [ -n "$REG_RESPONSE" ]; then
    70→      REG_SUCCESS="$(echo "$REG_RESPONSE" | jq -r '.success // false')"
    71→      if [ "$REG_SUCCESS" = "true" ]; then
    72→        LOCUS_API_KEY="$(echo "$REG_RESPONSE" | jq -r '.data.apiKey')"
    73→        LOCUS_OWNER_KEY="$(echo "$REG_RESPONSE" | jq -r '.data.ownerPrivateKey')"
    74→        LOCUS_WALLET="$(echo "$REG_RESPONSE" | jq -r '.data.ownerAddress')"
    75→        LOCUS_CLAIM_URL="$(echo "$REG_RESPONSE" | jq -r '.data.claimUrl')"
    76→        LOCUS_WALLET_STATUS="$(echo "$REG_RESPONSE" | jq -r '.data.walletStatus')"
    77→
    78→        export LOCUS_API_KEY
    79→
    80→        # Save credentials (they appear only once!)
    81→        echo "$REG_RESPONSE" | jq '.data' > "$LOCUS_CREDS_FILE"
    82→        chmod 600 "$LOCUS_CREDS_FILE"
    83→
    84→        echo "[init] Locus self-registration successful!"
    85→        echo "[init]   API Key:  ${LOCUS_API_KEY:0:20}..."
    86→        echo "[init]   Wallet:   $LOCUS_WALLET"
    87→        echo "[init]   Status:   $LOCUS_WALLET_STATUS"
    88→        echo "[init]   Claim:    $LOCUS_CLAIM_URL"
    89→        echo "[init]   IMPORTANT: Fund wallet with USDC on Base to enable payments"
    90→
    91→        # Wait for wallet deployment
    92→        if [ "$LOCUS_WALLET_STATUS" = "deploying" ]; then
    93→          echo "[init] Waiting for wallet deployment..."
    94→          for i in $(seq 1 30); do
    95→            sleep 2
    96→            STATUS_RESP="$(curl -sf https://beta-api.paywithlocus.com/api/status \
    97→              -H "Authorization: Bearer $LOCUS_API_KEY" 2>/dev/null)" || continue
    98→            WALLET_STATUS="$(echo "$STATUS_RESP" | jq -r '.data.walletStatus // .walletStatus // "unknown"')"
    99→            if [ "$WALLET_STATUS" = "deployed" ]; then
   100→              echo "[init] Wallet deployed!"
   101→              break
   102→            fi
   103→            echo "[init]   ...wallet status: $WALLET_STATUS (attempt $i/30)"
   104→          done
   105→        fi
   106→      else
   107→        echo "[init] WARNING: Locus registration returned success=false"
   108→        echo "[init] Response: $REG_RESPONSE"
   109→      fi
   110→    fi
   111→  fi
   112→fi
   113→
   114→# ── Write Locus credentials to filesystem ────────────────────────────────────
   115→if [ -n "$LOCUS_API_KEY" ]; then
   116→  echo "[init] Writing Locus credentials..."
   117→  for dir in /root/.config/locus /home/node/.config/locus /data/.config/locus; do
   118→    mkdir -p "$dir"
   119→    cat > "$dir/credentials.json" <<EOF
   120→{
   121→  "api_key": "$LOCUS_API_KEY",
   122→  "api_base": "https://beta-api.paywithlocus.com/api"
   123→}
   124→EOF
   125→  done
   126→else
   127→  echo "[init] WARNING: No Locus API key — agent cannot pay for services"
   128→fi
   129→
   130→# ── Configure inference provider ─────────────────────────────────────────────
   131→# Priority: Venice API key > Locus-wrapped OpenAI as bootstrap provider
   132→if [ -n "$VENICE_API_KEY" ]; then
   133→  echo "[init] Venice API key provided — using Venice for inference"
   134→else
   135→  if [ -n "$LOCUS_API_KEY" ]; then
   136→    # Use Locus-wrapped OpenAI as the bootstrap inference provider
   137→    # OpenClaw sees this as a standard OpenAI-compatible endpoint
   138→    echo "[init] No Venice key — using Locus-wrapped OpenAI for bootstrap inference"
   139→    export OPENAI_API_KEY="$LOCUS_API_KEY"
   140→    export OPENAI_BASE_URL="https://beta-api.paywithlocus.com/api/wrapped/openai"
   141→    export OPENCLAW_MODEL="${OPENCLAW_MODEL:-openai/gpt-4o-mini}"
   142→  else
   143→    echo "[init] WARNING: No inference provider available"
   144→    echo "[init] Set VENICE_API_KEY or LOCUS_API_KEY in .env"
   145→    echo "[init] Agent will boot but cannot process requests until a provider is configured"
   146→  fi
   147→fi
   148→
   149→# ── Render OpenClaw config from template (first boot only) ───────────────────
   150→if [ ! -s "$OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR/openclaw.json" ]; then
   151→  mkdir -p "$OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR"
   152→  envsubst < /app/custom/config/openclaw.template.json > "$OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR/openclaw.json"
   153→  echo "[init] Rendered openclaw.json from template"
   154→else
   155→  echo "[init] openclaw.json already exists — skipping template render"
   156→fi
   157→
   158→# ── Copy workspace files (first boot only) ──────────────────────────────────
   159→if [ ! -d "$OPENCLAW_WORKSPACE_DIR" ]; then
   160→  echo "[init] Setting up workspace..."
   161→  mkdir -p "$OPENCLAW_WORKSPACE_DIR"
   162→  cp -r /app/custom/workspaces/agent/* "$OPENCLAW_WORKSPACE_DIR/"
   163→
   164→  # Render any templates in workspace files
   165→  for f in "$OPENCLAW_WORKSPACE_DIR"/*.md; do
   166→    if [ -f "$f" ]; then
   167→      envsubst < "$f" > "$f.tmp" && mv "$f.tmp" "$f"
   168→    fi
   169→  done
   170→
   171→  echo "[init] Workspace ready at $OPENCLAW_WORKSPACE_DIR"
   172→else
   173→  echo "[init] Workspace already exists at $OPENCLAW_WORKSPACE_DIR"
   174→fi
   175→
   176→# ── Telegram channel ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   177→# Patch Telegram config into openclaw.json (same approach as farclaw/farcon-claw).
   178→# TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN is read from env by OpenClaw.
   179→# TELEGRAM_ALLOWED_USERS sets allowlist mode so no manual pairing is needed.
   180→if [ -n "${TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN:-}" ]; then
   181→  export TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN
   182→
   183→  # Patch openclaw.json to set dmPolicy and allowFrom
   184→  TELEGRAM_ALLOWED_USERS="${TELEGRAM_ALLOWED_USERS:-}" \
   185→  node <<'PATCH_EOF'
   186→const fs = require("fs");
   187→const configPath = process.env.OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR + "/openclaw.json";
   188→if (!fs.existsSync(configPath)) process.exit(0);
   189→
   190→const config = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(configPath, "utf8"));
   191→config.channels = config.channels || {};
   192→config.channels.telegram = config.channels.telegram || {};
   193→config.channels.telegram.enabled = true;
   194→
   195→const users = (process.env.TELEGRAM_ALLOWED_USERS || "")
   196→  .split(",")
   197→  .map(s => s.trim())
   198→  .filter(Boolean);
   199→
   200→if (users.length > 0) {
   201→  config.channels.telegram.dmPolicy = "allowlist";
   202→  config.channels.telegram.allowFrom = users;
   203→  console.log("[init] Telegram: configured (allowlist mode, users: " + users.join(", ") + ")");
   204→} else {
   205→  config.channels.telegram.dmPolicy = "paired";
   206→  config.channels.telegram.allowFrom = [];
   207→  console.log("[init] Telegram: configured (pairing mode)");
   208→}
   209→
   210→fs.writeFileSync(configPath, JSON.stringify(config, null, 2) + "\n", "utf8");
   211→PATCH_EOF
   212→else
   213→  echo "[init] Telegram: not configured (set TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN in .env)"
   214→fi
   215→
   216→echo "--------------------------------------------"
   217→echo "[init] Agent:    $AGENT_NAME"
   218→if [ -n "$VENICE_API_KEY" ]; then
   219→  echo "[init] Provider: Venice ($VENICE_MODEL)"
   220→else
   221→  echo "[init] Provider: Locus-wrapped OpenAI (bootstrap)"
   222→fi
   223→echo "[init] Locus:    $([ -n "$LOCUS_API_KEY" ] && echo "configured (${LOCUS_API_KEY:0:15}...)" || echo 'NOT configured')"
   224→echo "[init] Telegram: $([ -n "${TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN:-}" ] && echo 'configured' || echo 'not configured')"
   225→echo "--------------------------------------------"
   226→
   227→# ── Patch upstream entrypoint for single-container mode ──────────────────────
   228→ENTRYPOINT="/app/scripts/entrypoint.sh"
   229→if [ -f "$ENTRYPOINT" ]; then
   230→  sed -i '/# Browser sidecar proxy/,/^[[:space:]]*}$/d' "$ENTRYPOINT"
   231→  echo "[init] Patched entrypoint (removed browser sidecar block)"
   232→fi
   233→
   234→echo "[init] Handing off to OpenClaw entrypoint..."
   235→exec "$ENTRYPOINT"
   236→

1→# Lido
     2→LIDO_STETH_ADDRESS=

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me create a detailed summary document:

## Comprehensive Codebase Analysis: Synthesis Hack

Based on my thorough exploration of the codebase, here's what currently exists and what would need to change for a Celo + Self + Privy microlending pivot:

---

### 1. MONOREPO STRUCTURE

**Current Setup:**
- **Root:** `synthesis-hack` (pnpm workspaces + Turbo)
  - `apps/web/` — Next.js 16 frontend (React 19)
  - `apps/agent/` — Express.js runtime for OpenClaw agents
  - `packages/contracts/` — Foundry Solidity contracts (Base mainnet)
  - `packages/shared/` — Empty placeholder for shared types

**Package manager:** pnpm 9.15.0  
**Build tool:** Turbo v2  
**Current focus:** Base mainnet (not Celo), stETH yield (not microlending)

---

### 2. WEB APP STRUCTURE (`apps/web/`)

**Tech Stack:**
- Next.js 16.1.7 with App Router
- React 19.2.3
- Wagmi 3.5.0 for blockchain interaction
- Reown AppKit 1.8.19 for wallet connection (replaces WalletConnect)
- TailwindCSS 4 + custom CSS
- React Three Fiber + Drei for 3D (hero section has interactive Macintosh)

**Pages:**
- `/` — Landing page with hero section, specs, how-it-works
- `/dashboard` — Agent deployment & monitoring (requires wallet connection)

**API Routes (Next.js `/api/agent/`):**
- `POST /api/agent/deploy` — Deploys agent to Railway via GraphQL API
  - Takes: agentName, veniceModel, spendingCap, dailyLimit, optional Telegram config
  - Returns: projectId, serviceId, environmentId, domain, setupPassword
  - Creates service, env vars, volume, domain in Railway
  
- `GET /api/agent/deploy/status` — Polls deployment status
  - Extracts wallet address from deployment logs
  - Returns: status, deploymentId, walletAddress
  
- `POST /api/agent/register` — Locus agent self-registration
  - Calls Locus beta API to register agent & create wallet
  - Returns: apiKey, ownerPrivateKey, ownerAddress, walletId, claimUrl
  
- `GET /api/agent/status` — Gets wallet status from Locus
- `GET /api/agent/balance` — Gets wallet balance from Locus

**Components:**
- `Header` — Nav with wallet button (appkit-button)
- `Hero` — Dynamic 3D Macintosh scene (lazy-loaded, SSR off)
- `Specs` — Feature grid
- `HowItWorks` — Process flow
- `DeployAgent` — 4-step deployment flow:
  1. Configure (name, model, spending caps, optional Telegram)
  2. Deploy (to Railway)
  3. Fund (send USDC to agent wallet on Base)
  4. Monitor (view balance, yield accrual, fund options)
- `AgentMonitor` — Dashboard showing:
  - USDC balance, stETH staked, Lido APY, daily yield
  - Projected yield (accrued, daily, monthly, annual)
  - "Stake to Lido" and "Top Up Balance" actions (UI only, no real staking)

**Hooks:**
- `useAgentDeploy()` — Manages deployment state & polling
  - `deployAgent()` — POST to /api/agent/deploy
  - `pollDeployStatus()` — Polls status every 5s for ~5 mins
  - `getAgentWallet()` — Fetches wallet from agent /health endpoint

**Libraries:**
- `lib/railway.ts` — GraphQL queries to Railway API (createService, setEnvVars, createVolume, createDomain, getDeploymentStatus, getLatestDeployment, getDeploymentLogs)
- `lib/locus.ts` — Locus beta API client (registerAgent, getWalletStatus, getWalletBalance)
- `config/index.tsx` — Wagmi adapter setup for Base + BaseSepolia, Reown AppKit config

**Styling:**
- Custom CSS variables: `--font-sans`, `--font-serif`, `--font-geist-mono`
- Tailwind with custom colors: `cream`, `cream-dark`, `ink`, `ink-light`, `ink-lighter`
- No component library (just custom button with shadcn-like structure)

---

### 3. AGENT SETUP (`apps/agent/`)

**Container:** Dockerfile based on `coollabsio/openclaw:latest` (Debian)  
**Runtime:** Node.js with Express + OpenClaw gateway

**Entry Point:** `openclaw-init.sh` (bash script)
- Generates random gateway token if not provided
- **Locus self-registration flow:**
  - If no LOCUS_API_KEY env var: POST to `https://beta-api.paywithlocus.com/api/register`
  - Retries 5x with exponential backoff (5s, 10s, 20s, etc.)
  - Saves credentials to `/data/.locus-credentials.json` for persistence
  - Waits up to 60s for wallet deployment
- **Inference provider priority:**
  1. VENICE_API_KEY (direct Venice)
  2. Locus-wrapped OpenAI (fallback via `https://beta-api.paywithlocus.com/api/wrapped/openai`)
- Renders `openclaw.json` config from template
- Patches Telegram config if TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN provided
- Starts OpenClaw gateway via `npx openclaw gateway`

**Agent Runtime** (`src/index.ts`):
- Express.js server on port 3001
- Endpoints:
  - `GET /health` — Returns `{ ok: true, agents: count }`
  - `GET /agents` — Lists all deployed agents
  - `GET /agents/:name` — Agent status
  - `POST /agents/deploy` — Takes agentName, veniceModel, locusApiKey, ownerAddress, ownerPrivateKey, spendingCap, dailyLimit
    - Generates OpenClaw config, Locus credentials file, bootstrap & locus-payments skills
    - Spawns OpenClaw as child process
    - Returns agentId, walletAddress, status, workspace path
  - `POST /agents/:name/stop` — Kill agent process

**Agent Workspace** (`workspaces/agent/`):
- `IDENTITY.md` — Agent prompt: "You are an autonomous AI agent that funds its own operations from stETH yield"
- `SOUL.md` — Safety guardrails (spending limits, privacy, error handling)
- Skills directory (populated dynamically):
  - `locus-payments/SKILL.md` — Check balance, send USDC, call wrapped APIs
  - `bootstrap/SKILL.md` — On first run: check balance, acquire Venice key, test, report status

**Config Template** (`config/openclaw.template.json`):
- Configures Venice as inference provider
- Sets up Locus credentials

**Environment Variables:**
- `AGENT_NAME` — Name (used in logs, workspace dir)
- `VENICE_MODEL` — Model ID (default: `venice/llama-3.3-70b`)
- `VENICE_API_KEY` — Optional direct Venice key
- `LOCUS_API_KEY` — Optional; if empty, self-register
- `SETUP_PASSWORD` — Random password for setup
- `TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN` — Optional Telegram channel
- `TELEGRAM_ALLOWED_USERS` — Comma-separated allowlist

**Deployment Target:** Railway (via web app API)
- Creates unique service per agent
- Root directory: `apps/agent`
- Builds from GitHub repo + Dockerfile

---

### 4. SMART CONTRACTS (`packages/contracts/`)

**Framework:** Foundry (Solc 0.8.24)  
**Network:** Base mainnet  
**Pattern:** EIP-1167 minimal proxies

**Contracts:**

1. **YieldVault.sol** — Per-user vault
   - Holds wstETH (0xc1CBa3fCea344f92D9239c08C0568f6F2F0ee452)
   - Tracks principal in stETH units (via Chainlink wstETH/stETH rate)
   - Owner deposits/withdraws principal and yield
   - Agent withdraws configurable % of accrued yield on cooldown
   - Rate oracle: Chainlink aggregator (0xB88BAc61a4Ca37C43a3725912B1f472c9A5bc061)
   - Constants: 25-hour oracle staleness, 0.5–1.5x rate bounds
   - Safety: Reentrancy guard, pause mechanism (factory + vault level), emergency exit

2. **YieldVaultFactory.sol** — Singleton factory
   - Deploys YieldVault clones
   - One vault per owner
   - Tracks agent → vaults mapping
   - Max 50 vaults per agent
   - Factory admin can globally pause all vaults
   - Emits events: VaultCreated, AgentUpdated, FactoryPaused/Unpaused

3. **IAggregatorV3.sol** — Chainlink price feed interface

**Key Design:**
- Principal is **structurally locked** — agent can never access principal, only yield
- Yield computed on-demand via oracle rate, never minted as new tokens
- Agent withdrawals subject to time-based cooldown (configurable per vault)
- Owner has full control (withdraw, pause, emergency exit)

**Tests:** Fork tests against Base mainnet (foundry.toml configured)  
**Deploy Script:** `script/Deploy.s.sol` — Deploys factory + implementation, logs addresses

---

### 5. KEY DEPENDENCIES & INTEGRATIONS

**Web App:**
```
@reown/appkit: ^1.8.19              (wallet connection)
@reown/appkit-adapter-wagmi: ^1.8.19
wagmi: ^3.5.0                        (blockchain interaction)
viem: ^2.47.4                        (Ethereum library)
@tanstack/react-query: ^5.90.21     (data fetching)
@react-three/fiber: ^9.5.0          (3D)
@react-three/drei: ^10.7.7
three: ^0.183.2
next: 16.1.7
react: 19.2.3
tailwindcss: ^4
```

**Agent:**
```
express: ^5.2.1
openclaw: ^2026.3.13                (OpenClaw gateway/agent framework)
tsx: ^4.21.0                         (TypeScript runner)
tsup: ^8.5.1                         (bundler)
```

**Contracts:**
```
Foundry (Solc ^0.8.24)
@openzeppelin/contracts (IERC20, SafeERC20, Clones, Ownable, Math)
```

**APIs:**
- Railway GraphQL API (backboard.railway.com) — Deploy agents
- Locus beta API (beta-api.paywithlocus.com) — Agent wallets, payments
- Venice API (api.venice.ai) — Inference
- Chainlink (on-chain oracle) — wstETH/stETH rate

---

### 6. WHAT WOULD NEED TO CHANGE FOR CELO + SELF + PRIVY MICROLENDING

#### **Keep / Reuse:**
1. **Web app foundation** — Next.js 16, Wagmi/Viem, TailwindCSS, component structure
2. **Agent runtime architecture** — Express + OpenClaw remains solid for Celo-based agents
3. **Deployment pipeline** — Railway integration for agent scaling
4. **3D hero section** — Already built, no need to change
5. **API route structure** — Can adapt /api/agent/* for Celo microlending flows
6. **Config/credential patterns** — Self-registration model can work for Celo

#### **Replace / Rebuild:**
1. **Smart Contracts** — Entire contract suite changes:
   - `YieldVault.sol` → Microloan vault (borrow/repay logic)
   - `YieldVaultFactory.sol` → Microloan factory
   - Add collateral management (Self integration)
   - Add liquidity module (CELO + stables)
   - Remove wstETH oracle, add CELO/stablecoin oracle

2. **Agent Skills** — New skill categories:
   - ~~locus-payments~~ → Self payments or native CELO transfers
   - ~~bootstrap~~ → New: loan application, collateral posting, repayment scheduling
   - Add: credit scoring / reputation tracking

3. **Wallet Integration:**
   - Replace Locus beta with Privy for embedded wallets
   - Support CELO chain instead of Base
   - Update Railway deployment to inject Privy keys/configs

4. **Frontend Pages:**
   - `/dashboard` → Borrower app (apply for loan, post collateral, monitor repayment)
   - New: Lender dashboard (deposit, earn interest, track loans)
   - New: Loan marketplace (browse, bid on rates)
   - Update network config: Celo mainnet + Alfajores testnet

5. **API Routes:**
   - Replace `/api/agent/deploy` logic with Celo loan origination
   - New routes for loan application, collateral validation, repayment triggers
   - Connect to Self for collateral scoring

6. **Environment Variables:**
   - Remove: VENICE_MODEL, LOCUS_API_KEY, Lido addresses
   - Add: CELO_RPC_URL, SELF_API_KEY, PRIVY_APP_ID, PRIVY_PUBLIC_KEY

7. **Agent Bootstrap:**
   - Remove Venice/Locus wrappers
   - Bootstrap: Connect to Privy, post collateral, monitor loan status
   - Autonomous repayment execution from loan yields

---

### 7. TODAY'S STATE: WHAT EXISTS

**LIVE NOW:**
- ✅ Full web UI for agent deployment (4-step flow)
- ✅ Railway integration for containerized agents
- ✅ Locus self-registration (agents create own wallets autonomously)
- ✅ OpenClaw agent framework (Venice + wrapped APIs)
- ✅ YieldVault contracts (stETH yield → agent USDC on Base)
- ✅ 3D hero section (retro Macintosh)
- ✅ Wallet connection (Reown AppKit)
- ✅ Responsive design with custom typography

**NOT IMPLEMENTED (UI/Mock Only):**
- AgentMonitor "Stake to Lido" — UI only, no real staking
- AgentMonitor "Top Up Balance" — Sends USDC but balance doesn't persist
- Yield accrual in monitor — Simulated (every 10s = 1 hour of yield)
- Agent health checks for wallet address — Agent must return walletAddress in /health response

**Ready for Pivot:**
- Contract structure is sound and audited (per commit history)
- Agent runtime is flexible (can support new providers)
- Web app is modular (can swap out components by page)
- Deployment pipeline works (proven with Railway + Locus)

---

### 8. FILE PATHS (ABSOLUTE)

**Web App:**

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me create a detailed summary document:

## Comprehensive Codebase Analysis: Synthesis Hack

Based on my thorough exploration of the codebase, here's what currently exists and what would need to change for a Celo + Self + Privy microlending pivot:

---

### 1. MONOREPO STRUCTURE

**Current Setup:**
- **Root:** `synthesis-hack` (pnpm workspaces + Turbo)
  - `apps/web/` — Next.js 16 frontend (React 19)
  - `apps/agent/` — Express.js runtime for OpenClaw agents
  - `packages/contracts/` — Foundry Solidity contracts (Base mainnet)
  - `packages/shared/` — Empty placeholder for shared types

**Package manager:** pnpm 9.15.0  
**Build tool:** Turbo v2  
**Current focus:** Base mainnet (not Celo), stETH yield (not microlending)

---

### 2. WEB APP STRUCTURE (`apps/web/`)

**Tech Stack:**
- Next.js 16.1.7 with App Router
- React 19.2.3
- Wagmi 3.5.0 for blockchain interaction
- Reown AppKit 1.8.19 for wallet connection (replaces WalletConnect)
- TailwindCSS 4 + custom CSS
- React Three Fiber + Drei for 3D (hero section has interactive Macintosh)

**Pages:**
- `/` — Landing page with hero section, specs, how-it-works
- `/dashboard` — Agent deployment & monitoring (requires wallet connection)

**API Routes (Next.js `/api/agent/`):**
- `POST /api/agent/deploy` — Deploys agent to Railway via GraphQL API
  - Takes: agentName, veniceModel, spendingCap, dailyLimit, optional Telegram config
  - Returns: projectId, serviceId, environmentId, domain, setupPassword
  - Creates service, env vars, volume, domain in Railway
  
- `GET /api/agent/deploy/status` — Polls deployment status
  - Extracts wallet address from deployment logs
  - Returns: status, deploymentId, walletAddress
  
- `POST /api/agent/register` — Locus agent self-registration
  - Calls Locus beta API to register agent & create wallet
  - Returns: apiKey, ownerPrivateKey, ownerAddress, walletId, claimUrl
  
- `GET /api/agent/status` — Gets wallet status from Locus
- `GET /api/agent/balance` — Gets wallet balance from Locus

**Components:**
- `Header` — Nav with wallet button (appkit-button)
- `Hero` — Dynamic 3D Macintosh scene (lazy-loaded, SSR off)
- `Specs` — Feature grid
- `HowItWorks` — Process flow
- `DeployAgent` — 4-step deployment flow:
  1. Configure (name, model, spending caps, optional Telegram)
  2. Deploy (to Railway)
  3. Fund (send USDC to agent wallet on Base)
  4. Monitor (view balance, yield accrual, fund options)
- `AgentMonitor` — Dashboard showing:
  - USDC balance, stETH staked, Lido APY, daily yield
  - Projected yield (accrued, daily, monthly, annual)
  - "Stake to Lido" and "Top Up Balance" actions (UI only, no real staking)

**Hooks:**
- `useAgentDeploy()` — Manages deployment state & polling
  - `deployAgent()` — POST to /api/agent/deploy
  - `pollDeployStatus()` — Polls status every 5s for ~5 mins
  - `getAgentWallet()` — Fetches wallet from agent /health endpoint

**Libraries:**
- `lib/railway.ts` — GraphQL queries to Railway API (createService, setEnvVars, createVolume, createDomain, getDeploymentStatus, getLatestDeployment, getDeploymentLogs)
- `lib/locus.ts` — Locus beta API client (registerAgent, getWalletStatus, getWalletBalance)
- `config/index.tsx` — Wagmi adapter setup for Base + BaseSepolia, Reown AppKit config

**Styling:**
- Custom CSS variables: `--font-sans`, `--font-serif`, `--font-geist-mono`
- Tailwind with custom colors: `cream`, `cream-dark`, `ink`, `ink-light`, `ink-lighter`
- No component library (just custom button with shadcn-like structure)

---

### 3. AGENT SETUP (`apps/agent/`)

**Container:** Dockerfile based on `coollabsio/openclaw:latest` (Debian)  
**Runtime:** Node.js with Express + OpenClaw gateway

**Entry Point:** `openclaw-init.sh` (bash script)
- Generates random gateway token if not provided
- **Locus self-registration flow:**
  - If no LOCUS_API_KEY env var: POST to `https://beta-api.paywithlocus.com/api/register`
  - Retries 5x with exponential backoff (5s, 10s, 20s, etc.)
  - Saves credentials to `/data/.locus-credentials.json` for persistence
  - Waits up to 60s for wallet deployment
- **Inference provider priority:**
  1. VENICE_API_KEY (direct Venice)
  2. Locus-wrapped OpenAI (fallback via `https://beta-api.paywithlocus.com/api/wrapped/openai`)
- Renders `openclaw.json` config from template
- Patches Telegram config if TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN provided
- Starts OpenClaw gateway via `npx openclaw gateway`

**Agent Runtime** (`src/index.ts`):
- Express.js server on port 3001
- Endpoints:
  - `GET /health` — Returns `{ ok: true, agents: count }`
  - `GET /agents` — Lists all deployed agents
  - `GET /agents/:name` — Agent status
  - `POST /agents/deploy` — Takes agentName, veniceModel, locusApiKey, ownerAddress, ownerPrivateKey, spendingCap, dailyLimit
    - Generates OpenClaw config, Locus credentials file, bootstrap & locus-payments skills
    - Spawns OpenClaw as child process
    - Returns agentId, walletAddress, status, workspace path
  - `POST /agents/:name/stop` — Kill agent process

**Agent Workspace** (`workspaces/agent/`):
- `IDENTITY.md` — Agent prompt: "You are an autonomous AI agent that funds its own operations from stETH yield"
- `SOUL.md` — Safety guardrails (spending limits, privacy, error handling)
- Skills directory (populated dynamically):
  - `locus-payments/SKILL.md` — Check balance, send USDC, call wrapped APIs
  - `bootstrap/SKILL.md` — On first run: check balance, acquire Venice key, test, report status

**Config Template** (`config/openclaw.template.json`):
- Configures Venice as inference provider
- Sets up Locus credentials

**Environment Variables:**
- `AGENT_NAME` — Name (used in logs, workspace dir)
- `VENICE_MODEL` — Model ID (default: `venice/llama-3.3-70b`)
- `VENICE_API_KEY` — Optional direct Venice key
- `LOCUS_API_KEY` — Optional; if empty, self-register
- `SETUP_PASSWORD` — Random password for setup
- `TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN` — Optional Telegram channel
- `TELEGRAM_ALLOWED_USERS` — Comma-separated allowlist

**Deployment Target:** Railway (via web app API)
- Creates unique service per agent
- Root directory: `apps/agent`
- Builds from GitHub repo + Dockerfile

---

### 4. SMART CONTRACTS (`packages/contracts/`)

**Framework:** Foundry (Solc 0.8.24)  
**Network:** Base mainnet  
**Pattern:** EIP-1167 minimal proxies

**Contracts:**

1. **YieldVault.sol** — Per-user vault
   - Holds wstETH (0xc1CBa3fCea344f92D9239c08C0568f6F2F0ee452)
   - Tracks principal in stETH units (via Chainlink wstETH/stETH rate)
   - Owner deposits/withdraws principal and yield
   - Agent withdraws configurable % of accrued yield on cooldown
   - Rate oracle: Chainlink aggregator (0xB88BAc61a4Ca37C43a3725912B1f472c9A5bc061)
   - Constants: 25-hour oracle staleness, 0.5–1.5x rate bounds
   - Safety: Reentrancy guard, pause mechanism (factory + vault level), emergency exit

2. **YieldVaultFactory.sol** — Singleton factory
   - Deploys YieldVault clones
   - One vault per owner
   - Tracks agent → vaults mapping
   - Max 50 vaults per agent
   - Factory admin can globally pause all vaults
   - Emits events: VaultCreated, AgentUpdated, FactoryPaused/Unpaused

3. **IAggregatorV3.sol** — Chainlink price feed interface

**Key Design:**
- Principal is **structurally locked** — agent can never access principal, only yield
- Yield computed on-demand via oracle rate, never minted as new tokens
- Agent withdrawals subject to time-based cooldown (configurable per vault)
- Owner has full control (withdraw, pause, emergency exit)

**Tests:** Fork tests against Base mainnet (foundry.toml configured)  
**Deploy Script:** `script/Deploy.s.sol` — Deploys factory + implementation, logs addresses

---

### 5. KEY DEPENDENCIES & INTEGRATIONS

**Web App:**
```
@reown/appkit: ^1.8.19              (wallet connection)
@reown/appkit-adapter-wagmi: ^1.8.19
wagmi: ^3.5.0                        (blockchain interaction)
viem: ^2.47.4                        (Ethereum library)
@tanstack/react-query: ^5.90.21     (data fetching)
@react-three/fiber: ^9.5.0          (3D)
@react-three/drei: ^10.7.7
three: ^0.183.2
next: 16.1.7
react: 19.2.3
tailwindcss: ^4
```

**Agent:**
```
express: ^5.2.1
openclaw: ^2026.3.13                (OpenClaw gateway/agent framework)
tsx: ^4.21.0                         (TypeScript runner)
tsup: ^8.5.1                         (bundler)
```

**Contracts:**
```
Foundry (Solc ^0.8.24)
@openzeppelin/contracts (IERC20, SafeERC20, Clones, Ownable, Math)
```

**APIs:**
- Railway GraphQL API (backboard.railway.com) — Deploy agents
- Locus beta API (beta-api.paywithlocus.com) — Agent wallets, payments
- Venice API (api.venice.ai) — Inference
- Chainlink (on-chain oracle) — wstETH/stETH rate

---

### 6. WHAT WOULD NEED TO CHANGE FOR CELO + SELF + PRIVY MICROLENDING

#### **Keep / Reuse:**
1. **Web app foundation** — Next.js 16, Wagmi/Viem, TailwindCSS, component structure
2. **Agent runtime architecture** — Express + OpenClaw remains solid for Celo-based agents
3. **Deployment pipeline** — Railway integration for agent scaling
4. **3D hero section** — Already built, no need to change
5. **API route structure** — Can adapt /api/agent/* for Celo microlending flows
6. **Config/credential patterns** — Self-registration model can work for Celo

#### **Replace / Rebuild:**
1. **Smart Contracts** — Entire contract suite changes:
   - `YieldVault.sol` → Microloan vault (borrow/repay logic)
   - `YieldVaultFactory.sol` → Microloan factory
   - Add collateral management (Self integration)
   - Add liquidity module (CELO + stables)
   - Remove wstETH oracle, add CELO/stablecoin oracle

2. **Agent Skills** — New skill categories:
   - ~~locus-payments~~ → Self payments or native CELO transfers
   - ~~bootstrap~~ → New: loan application, collateral posting, repayment scheduling
   - Add: credit scoring / reputation tracking

3. **Wallet Integration:**
   - Replace Locus beta with Privy for embedded wallets
   - Support CELO chain instead of Base
   - Update Railway deployment to inject Privy keys/configs

4. **Frontend Pages:**
   - `/dashboard` → Borrower app (apply for loan, post collateral, monitor repayment)
   - New: Lender dashboard (deposit, earn interest, track loans)
   - New: Loan marketplace (browse, bid on rates)
   - Update network config: Celo mainnet + Alfajores testnet

5. **API Routes:**
   - Replace `/api/agent/deploy` logic with Celo loan origination
   - New routes for loan application, collateral validation, repayment triggers
   - Connect to Self for collateral scoring

6. **Environment Variables:**
   - Remove: VENICE_MODEL, LOCUS_API_KEY, Lido addresses
   - Add: CELO_RPC_URL, SELF_API_KEY, PRIVY_APP_ID, PRIVY_PUBLIC_KEY

7. **Agent Bootstrap:**
   - Remove Venice/Locus wrappers
   - Bootstrap: Connect to Privy, post collateral, monitor loan status
   - Autonomous repayment execution from loan yields

---

### 7. TODAY'S STATE: WHAT EXISTS

**LIVE NOW:**
- ✅ Full web UI for agent deployment (4-step flow)
- ✅ Railway integration for containerized agents
- ✅ Locus self-registration (agents create own wallets autonomously)
- ✅ OpenClaw agent framework (Venice + wrapped APIs)
- ✅ YieldVault contracts (stETH yield → agent USDC on Base)
- ✅ 3D hero section (retro Macintosh)
- ✅ Wallet connection (Reown AppKit)
- ✅ Responsive design with custom typography

**NOT IMPLEMENTED (UI/Mock Only):**
- AgentMonitor "Stake to Lido" — UI only, no real staking
- AgentMonitor "Top Up Balance" — Sends USDC but balance doesn't persist
- Yield accrual in monitor — Simulated (every 10s = 1 hour of yield)
- Agent health checks for wallet address — Agent must return walletAddress in /health response

**Ready for Pivot:**
- Contract structure is sound and audited (per commit history)
- Agent runtime is flexible (can support new providers)
- Web app is modular (can swap out components by page)
- Deployment pipeline works (proven with Railway + Locus)

---

### 8. FILE PATHS (ABSOLUTE)

**Web App:**
- `/Users/limone/Documents/builders-garden/synthesis-hack/apps/web/`
- Pages: `src/app/page.tsx`, `src/app/dashboard/page.tsx`, `src/app/layout.tsx`
- Components: `src/components/[deploy-agent, agent-monitor, hero, header, etc].tsx`
- API: `src/app/api/agent/[deploy, register, status, balance, deploy/status]/route.ts`
- Libraries: `src/lib/[railway.ts, locus.ts, utils.ts]`
- Config: `src/config/index.tsx`, `src/context/index.tsx`

**Agent:**
- `/Users/limone/Documents/builders-garden/synthesis-hack/apps/agent/`
- Runtime: `src/index.ts`
- Docker: `Dockerfile`, `openclaw-init.sh`
- Workspace: `workspaces/agent/[IDENTITY.md, SOUL.md]`, `workspaces/agent/skills/`
- Config template: `config/openclaw.template.json`

**Contracts:**
- `/Users/limone/Documents/builders-garden/synthesis-hack/packages/contracts/`
- Vaults: `src/[YieldVault.sol, YieldVaultFactory.sol]`
- Interfaces: `src/interfaces/IAggregatorV3.sol`
- Tests: `test/YieldVault.t.sol`
- Deploy: `script/Deploy.s.sol`

**Configuration:**
- Root: `package.json`, `pnpm-workspace.yaml`, `turbo.json`, `.env.example`
- Web: `tsconfig.json`, `apps/web/package.json`
- Agent: `apps/agent/package.json`, `apps/agent/.env.example`, `apps/agent/railway.json`
- Contracts: `packages/contracts/foundry.toml`, `packages/contracts/package.json`

---

This codebase is production-quality and well-architected for a rapid pivot. The separation of concerns (web, agent, contracts, shared) makes it straightforward to swap out the financial primitive (stETH yield → microloans) while keeping the agent orchestration and deployment infrastructure intact.
